seoTitle: Carramar vs Carrick — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Carramar and Carrick. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carramar and Carrick.
heading: Carramar vs Carrick — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carramar, New South Wales and Carrick, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carramar has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Carrick (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carramar or Carrick safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carramar has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Carrick scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carramar scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. Carrick scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carramar or Carrick?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carramar has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
